package repository

import (
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Anti-pattern: Repository returns *gorm.DB query builder instead of
// executing the query and returning concrete results.

type Comment struct {
	ID       string `gorm:"column:id"`
	PostID   string `gorm:"column:post_id"`
	AuthorID string `gorm:"column:author_id"`
	Body     string `gorm:"column:body"`
}

type CommentRepository struct {
	db *gorm.DB
}

// QueryByPost returns *gorm.DB instead of []Comment - anti-pattern.
func (r *CommentRepository) QueryByPost(postID string) *gorm.DB {
	return r.db.Where("post_id = ?", postID).Order("created_at ASC")
}

// QueryByAuthor returns *gorm.DB instead of []Comment - anti-pattern.
func (r *CommentRepository) QueryByAuthor(authorID string) *gorm.DB {
	return r.db.Where("author_id = ?", authorID)
}

// Recent returns a partially built *gorm.DB query - anti-pattern.
func (r *CommentRepository) Recent(limit int) *gorm.DB {
	return r.db.Order("created_at DESC").Limit(limit)
}

// Example misuse:
//
//	query := repo.QueryByPost("post-123")
//	query.Where("author_id = ?", "user-456").Find(&comments)
//
// The repository returns the gorm query builder, forcing the caller
// to know how to execute the query.
